consistency.class_match: The feature is correctly assigned to the 'monk' class.
consistency.level_appropriateness: Level 1 is standard for core class features.
consistency.name_and_slug: The name 'Patient Defense' is descriptive and consistent with the slug.

quality.description_clarity: The description is clear and directly references standard 5e actions ('Dodge action', 'bonus action', 'ki point').
quality.completeness: It includes all necessary mechanical components: cost (1 ki point), action type (bonus action), and effect (Dodge action).

mechanics.core_function: Grants a Monk the ability to gain the benefits of the Dodge action without using their main Action, by expending a resource (Ki).
mechanics.balance_assessment: This is the standard implementation of the Patient Defense feature from the Player's Handbook (PHB). It is well-balanced for its level, offering defensive utility at the cost of resource management.
mechanics.action_economy_impact: High impact on action economy, allowing a Monk to use their Action for an attack or other ability while still boosting AC/disadvantage on attacks against them.

analysis.data_archivist_verdict: Consistent, high-quality, and mechanically sound representation of the core Monk class feature.
